content:
Activity: Rewrite Each Logline Five Different Ways
1. Change the genre – How does the story shift if it’s a horror, comedy, noir, sci-fi, fantasy?
2. Change the protagonist – What if the lead was older, younger, a different gender, non-human?
3. Change the conflict – What if instead of a villain, the struggle was against nature, time, or the protagonist’s own mind?
4. Change the setting – What if it took place 1,000 years in the future, in feudal Japan, or in a dreamscape?
5. Change the ending – What if the story’s resolution was tragic, ambiguous, cyclical, or surreal?

2. Expanding an Idea (Different Story Structures)

Once you’ve settled on an idea, the next step is to develop it in different forms to see what structure best fits.

Activity: Expand Your Logline in Five Different Ways
1. The Pixar Story Spine
• Once upon a time…
• Every day…
• Until one day…
• Because of that… (repeat 2-3 times)
• Until finally…
• And ever since then…
context: From document: I’ve taken all of this into account and structured it into a logical… — chunk 3 (chunk 3/8)
